name: vpc-firewall-rules
title: GCP Firewall — VPC Firewall Rules
summary: Creates classic (network-global, stateful) VPC firewall rules with tag or service-account targeting, allow/deny actions, priorities, and optional logging.

governance.name: prefix
governance.description: Short organization or project prefix. First token of every resource name: {prefix}-{cloud}-{environment}-{resource-type}-{suffix}.

governance.name: environment
governance.description: Deployment environment identifier (e.g., dev, test, prod). Used in resource names.

governance.name: owner
governance.description: Team or individual that owns the deployed resources. Retained for convention parity (classic firewall rules are not labelable).

governance.name: cost_center
governance.description: Cost center code. Retained for convention parity (classic firewall rules are not labelable).

governance.name: additional_tags
governance.description: Retained for convention parity; classic VPC firewall rules do not support labels.

governance.name: name_suffix
governance.description: Final token of every resource name, used to disambiguate multiple instances of this module.

module.name: project_id
module.description: GCP project ID owning the network and rules.

module.name: network_name
module.description: Name (or self-link) of the VPC network the rules attach to. Supplied as a plain value — no framework dependency is implied.

module.name: rules
module.type: map(object({ description = optional(string) direction = string action = string priority = optional(number, 1000) source_ranges = optional(list(string), []) source_tags = optional(list(string), []) source_service_accounts = optional(list(string), []) destination_ranges = optional(list(string), []) target_tags = optional(list(string), []) target_service_accounts = optional(list(string), []) allow_deny = list(object({ protocol = string ports = optional(list(string), []) })) log_enabled = optional(bool, false) }))
module.description: Map of firewall rules keyed by logical name. GCP rules are NETWORK-GLOBAL and stateful; they target instances via network tags or service accounts (never both in one rule — GCP rejects mixing tags and service accounts). Implied rules: allow-egress-all and deny-ingress-all exist at priority 65535 in every VPC.

outputs.name: firewall_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical rule name to firewall rule ID. (GCP has no grouping resource; each rule is standalone, so firewall_ids == rule_ids.)

outputs.name: rule_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical rule name to firewall rule ID.

outputs.name: rule_self_links
outputs.description: Map of logical rule name to firewall rule self-link.
